programs.name: NH Medication Bridge Program
programs.what_it_does: This state-funded program provides financial support to cover prescription drug costs for eligible seniors. It bridges gaps in medication access beyond federal programs like Medicare Part D.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65+ residing in New Hampshire.
programs.whats_unique: Unlike standard federal pharmaceutical assistance, this is a NH-specific initiative using state funds to assist with medication affordability not fully covered elsewhere.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Covers all or part of prescription costs; exact value based on individual eligibility and drug needs.

programs.name: New Hampshire Partnership for Prescription
programs.what_it_does: This program partners to reduce prescription costs for seniors through state-supported discounts and assistance. It targets affordability for long-term medication needs.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ in NH with financial need for prescriptions.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific collaboration beyond federal options, focused on NH residents' pharmaceutical access.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Discounts on prescriptions; amount varies by participation and eligibility.

programs.name: Medical Assistance (NH Medicaid Waiver)
programs.what_it_does: Provides state and federal funds for long-term care alternatives like personal care, home health aides, respite, and durable medical equipment. Allows seniors to avoid nursing homes by receiving care at home.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ or disabled adults in NH requiring nursing home level of care, with income under $30,276/year and assets under $2,500.
programs.whats_unique: NH's waiver program extends beyond standard federal Medicaid by funding specific home-based services unique to state administration.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Covers costs of home care services; up to full coverage for eligible needs.

programs.name: REAP (Respite, Education, and Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: Offers community-based short-term support including respite for caregivers and resources for older adults. Helps reduce caregiver burden and supports family members.
programs.who_its_for: Older adults in NH, their caregivers, family members, and professionals working with seniors.
programs.whats_unique: NH-specific program providing targeted respite and education not universally available in other states.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Short-term support services; value in hours of respite or assistance provided.

programs.name: Easterseals NH Adult Day Services
programs.what_it_does: Provides daytime social engagement, physical activity, health services, and memory care in homelike settings. Coordinates transportation and supports aging in place.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors averaging age 76 in NH, including those needing memory care, across various abilities.
programs.whats_unique: State-operated through Easterseals NH with specialized sites like secure Manchester memory care, beyond generic federal programs.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Daily programs; affordable access promoting health and connection.

programs.name: Easterseals NH In-Home Care & Health Services
programs.what_it_does: Delivers affordable in-home nursing, personal hygiene, meal prep, and housekeeping as alternative to nursing homes. Can combine with other services for comprehensive support.
programs.who_its_for: Older adults and disabled individuals in NH seeking to age at home.
programs.whats_unique: NH-focused provider with 30+ years serving thousands, emphasizing person-centered care unique to local needs.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Hourly in-home services; cost-effective vs. institutional care.
